description: Formulation considerations for sub-AUD-dose disulfiram in a 503A compounding context. Anchored to the MINX-precedent ER-tablet protocol referenced in wiki/compounding-pharmacy-track.md.

current_commercial_formulation.name: Antabuse (disulfiram tablets)
current_commercial_formulation.limitations_for_sub_AUD_use: Tablet splitting to 125 mg is patient-doable but content uniformity drops below USP <905> (RSD ≤ 6%). To 62.5 mg, splitting is impractical. ER is not commercial.

compounding_formulation_options.matrix: lactose / microcrystalline cellulose blend; gelatin capsule shell
compounding_formulation_options.release_profile: immediate_release (same dissolution profile as commercial Antabuse)
compounding_formulation_options.PK_implication: Maintains commercial Cmax/AUC scaling at fractional dose. Cmax at 125 mg ≈ 0.5× of 250 mg Cmax. Acceptable starting protocol.
compounding_formulation_options.complexity: low — standard 503A capsule compounding (USP <795>); no formulation engineering needed
compounding_formulation_options.best_for: initial dose-titration phase + patient-individualized starting doses

compounding_formulation_options.matrix: glyceryl behenate (Compritol 888 ATO) + HPMC K100M; lipid-matrix design analogous to MINX 5 mg minoxidil ER
compounding_formulation_options.PK_implication: Reduces Cmax by ~50% while maintaining AUC. Sustains plasma DSF concentration in the 0.1-0.3 μM range (GSDMD-blockade plateau) without spiking into the 1-3 μM range (closer to NLRP3-palmitoylation IC50 territory). Could allow QD dosing of 125 mg/d with peak plasma below the cellular NLRP3 EC50 even at the highest end of inter-individual PK variability.
compounding_formulation_options.complexity: medium — requires formulation engineering + dissolution testing per USP <711>; analogous to MINX protocol described in wiki/compounding-pharmacy-track.md §Formulation engineering
compounding_formulation_options.best_for: long-term maintenance dosing in 503A-prescribed gout prophylaxis

compounding_formulation_options.matrix: wet-milled disulfiram particles (<5 μm); dispersed in standard capsule
compounding_formulation_options.PK_implication: Increases bioavailability via dissolution-rate enhancement (DSF aqueous solubility is poor, ~0.4 mg/L). Useful if dose-dependent fraction-absorbed shows under-absorption at low doses.
compounding_formulation_options.complexity: medium — requires particle-size verification (laser diffraction); some 503A pharmacies have capability, many don't
compounding_formulation_options.best_for: if low-dose IR shows unexpectedly low or variable absorption

compounding_formulation_options.matrix: standard capsule with HPMC-phthalate enteric coating; dissolves in duodenum not stomach
compounding_formulation_options.PK_implication: Bypasses gastric environment. Limited benefit for DSF (which is acid-stable). May help with food-effect variability.
compounding_formulation_options.best_for: patients with persistent GI side effects from immediate-release

quality_control_requirements.USP_711_dissolution: required for ER formulations; specifies dissolution profile (typically 10-30%/2h, 40-60%/6h, ≥80%/12h for 12h ER)
quality_control_requirements.USP_905_content_uniformity: required for all compounded solid dosage forms; RSD ≤ 6%
quality_control_requirements.USP_797_compounding_standards: general framework — sterile (not relevant for oral disulfiram)
quality_control_requirements.third_party_dissolution_lab: compounding pharmacy may outsource; cost ~$500-1500 per formulation tested
quality_control_requirements.long_term_stability: DSF is hygroscopic and decomposes in heat/light; standard pharmaceutical handling adequate; beyond-use date (BUD) per USP <795> guidance

503A_practical_path.phase_1: low-dose IR capsule, 62.5 or 125 mg/day, titrated from 25 mg. Simplest 503A workflow.
503A_practical_path.phase_2: ER lipid-matrix tablet at 100-125 mg/day. Requires formulation development + dissolution testing.
503A_practical_path.phase_3: Sub-AUD ER tablet formalized as a stable repurposed-indication product — could shift to 503B outsourcing-facility production if patient volumes support it.
503A_practical_path.timeline_estimate: Phase 1: weeks (existing 503A workflow). Phase 2: months (formulation development + dissolution testing). Phase 3: 1-2 years (regulatory + manufacturing scale).

compounding_pharmacy_candidates.selection_criteria: track record with ER formulations + low-dose repurposed indications; willingness to develop new formulation profile; relationship with rheumatology / functional-medicine prescriber network
compounding_pharmacy_candidates.verification_status: user_action_required — wiki/compounding-pharmacy-track.md §Open questions item 2 stays open
